text: Bats don’t see the night; they hear it. Using echolocation, bats emit high-frequency calls and listen for echoes. These echoes reveal the size, shape, and distance of objects, painting a sonic picture. Research shows bats can distinguish between different objects just by their sound. Their world is a symphony of sounds, allowing them to thrive in complete darkness. Curious about more animal superpowers? Like and follow for more!
